_note: Ground-truth expectations for the credential detector, per docs/specs/credential-lens.md. `expect_findings` = entries the detector must emit with kind='finding' (unambiguous leaks); `expect_inventory` = entries it must emit with kind='inventory' (ambiguous outbound, ADR-0002). Fixtures authored in isolation; the two outbound/field cases were reconciled to the settled v1 model (a token sent to its own API is legitimate auth = inventory, not a leak; field-based cross-method flow is a documented v1 known-miss).

fixtures.reason: env-var NAME as a string literal never reaches a sink
env-var NAME as a string literal never reaches a sink
env-var NAME as a string literal never reaches a sink
`return apiKey` is not a sink; the log arg is a string literal
proximity to a log is not dataflow
*_PATH env var is a pointer; writing to it / logging a status string is not a secret leak
*_PATH env var is a pointer, not a secret value
secret aliased through a binding into console.log — the canonical must-catch

aliased secret reaches an OUTBOUND request -> inventory in v1 (a token to its own API is legitimate auth; auth-vs-exfil needs destination-taint, a v2 refinement). Confirms aliasing is tracked all the way to the sink.

secret flows through an instance FIELD (this.secret) across two methods, then to an outbound sink -- field-based cross-method taint is a documented v1 intra-file known-miss (and the sink is outbound = inventory regardless)
